content.observe: One CPU means one thing happening at a time, no matter how you slice it. More cores means true parallel execution — if the work can actually be split.
content.fault: You tried to run four things on one core and called it parallelism. It isn't — it's just fast switching.

hotspots.label: Single-Core
hotspots.detail: One execution unit. Concurrency here is an illusion built from rapid switching.

hotspots.label: Multiprocessor
hotspots.detail: Multiple separate CPUs, each with its own execution hardware, sharing memory.

hotspots.label: Multicore
hotspots.detail: Multiple execution cores on a single chip, often sharing cache.
